title: Web 3D over-bright (DAM-R1b) REFUTED on opaque surfaces; CDP byte-faithful to GPU readback; compositor path + sky hue BOTH CLOSED at matched geometry (rnd 2)
repro: Same dam --deterministic frame read FOUR ways and compared per-ROI (harness: task-4 webprobe.mjs, patched throwaway dist that seeds ENV from ?env= and exposes window._mgb64). (2) CDP Page.captureScreenshot; (i-scene) GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_FRAME=N -> /tmp/webgpu_frame_N.ppm (offscreen scene target, pre-present GPU-buffer readback); (i-surface) GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE=N -> /tmp/webgpu_surface_N.ppm (post present-copy GPU-buffer readback). CDP is captured the instant the MEMFS dump file appears so all three web reads share one camera pose. Native baseline: build/ge007 --level dam --deterministic --screenshot-frame N (wgpu-native).

evidence.path: src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c (task-4 web2, frame 500, tight pose alignment): full-image mean-abs-diff CDP screenshot vs GPU-buffer readback = R 0.525 / G 0.499 / B 0.488; frame-invariant rock+ground ROIs are byte-identical between CDP and both GPU dumps. => Page.captureScreenshot introduces NO brightness lift; it reports the engine's rendered bytes. Hypothesis (a) 'CDP color-management artifact' is FALSE.

src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c (task-4 ROI means, frame 500; native f300 640x480 vs web f500 640x393, matched pose): native [32.0,32.6,34.3] vs web-CDP [30.9,31.5,33.2] vs web-dump [30.9,31.5,33.2] (web ~1 DARKER). ground: native [77.9,79.2,83.3] vs web [78.5,79.8,83.9] (web +0.6). gun/viewmodel: native [63.2,64.3,67.5] vs web [60.5..61.3] (web ~2 darker). Deltas are <=2-3 levels and go BOTH directions -> NO systematic over-bright. Hypothesis (b) 'genuine 3D over-bright divergence' is FALSE for a lift.

src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c:1510 (task-4 present-copy): web_dump_surface == web_dump_scene byte-for-byte (identical full-image diff vs CDP) -> the present-copy blit into the canvas swapchain is a pure LINEAR copy with no sRGB re-encode-on-store, independently corroborating the 2026-07-18 CORRECTION (both surfaces are linear BGRA8; the reverted sRGB fix bb4a824 was correctly a no-op).

src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c (task-4 sky-ROI, CORRECTED Fix round 1): the sky ROI is unreliable cross-frame not because of camera drift but because the sky itself is TIME-VARYING at a STATIC camera. Re-derived directly from the preserved capture bitmaps (roi.mjs re-run 2026-07-21): rock/ground are byte-identical frame-for-frame across the whole sweep ([32.0,32.6,34.3] / [77.9,79.2,83.3], zero variation f120-f350), proving the camera does not move. Meanwhile native dam sky-ROI mean climbs monotonically with NO input: f120 [39.2,54.3,76.4], f200 [46.8,60.1,80.3], f250 [56.9,68.0,85.7], f300 [62.1,72.1,88.6], f350 [72.1,80.3,94.5] -- roughly +18 to +33 levels/channel over 230 frames at a fixed pose. (The original 2026-07-20 text here cited different, non-reproducing numbers -- f120 [60.9,89.0,130.5], f200 [34.4,68.4,116.5], f350 [78.4,103.0,140.3] -- and attributed the swing to 'the drifting camera sweeping the rock silhouette'; neither the numbers nor the camera-drift mechanism survive re-derivation and are RETRACTED.) The prior ~7-11 (scene) / ~20 (sky) 'lift' was a cross-frame sky comparison made invalid by this time-varying sky term, not a render or capture defect. Within one pose (frame 500, the primary/clean run), web-CDP sky [36.5,64.7,105.1] ~= web-dump sky [36.4,64.7,105.0] (<=0.4 level); at a second pose (frame 250) CDP vs dump sky differs by ~2-3 levels ([61.9,84.4,118.6] vs [64.5,86.4,120.0]) -- noted rather than folded into a blanket 'CDP==dump'.

src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c (task-4 web1, 1 of 3 runs, Fix round 1 disclosure): the poll-for-dump-then-CDP-screenshot synchronization race FAILED in this run -- CDP sky [36.4,64.7,105.1] vs GPU-dump sky [72.6,92.9,124.6], full-image mean-abs-diff R 3.31 / G 2.95 / B 2.48 (vs R 0.53 / G 0.50 / B 0.49 on the clean run used for the closure table). This was undisclosed in the original 2026-07-20 closure. Even in this failed run, rock/ground stayed byte-identical CDP-vs-dump, so the failure is temporal (the two reads landed on different frames), not colorimetric -- the 'CDP is faithful to the buffer' conclusion survives, but the harness's capture synchronization is not fully reliable and should be re-verified (e.g. an assert on matched frame counters) before further use.

src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c (Task 6, residual (a) COMPOSITOR PATH -- CLOSED, n=1 pose). Headful Chrome on macOS, canvas pinned to a 640x480 backing store, engine GPU surface dump of frame 600, rAF pump frozen on dump detection so the presented frame stops advancing (freeze verified: two screencaptures 1.2 s apart are BYTE-IDENTICAL -- this proves TEMPORAL stability of that one captured frame, NOT pose coverage). macOS `screencapture` of the visible window = the true compositor/display path, 2x2 box-downsampled to the buffer's native 640x480 (verified 2x alignment, ox=0 oy=1). After correcting the capture's Display P3 profile to sRGB (see FID-0138): rock delta [+0.27,+0.16,+0.03], ground [+0.38,+0.11,-0.17], sky [+2.18,+1.48,+0.51] dHue +0.05 deg, skyhi [+1.52,+1.09,+0.28] dHue -0.07 deg, gun [+1.63,+1.43,+1.31] dHue +1.62 deg (rock/ground/gun all sit at saturation~0.06, where hue is numerically ill-conditioned and not meaningful there). => rock/ground agree to <=0.4 levels; sky/skyhi to <=2.2 levels with hue within 0.1 deg. gun is +1.6 levels and is NOT isolated: suspected cause is the 2x2 box-downsample alignment (ox=0 oy=1) landing badly on gun's high-frequency viewmodel content, not a render divergence -- and note the FID-0138 P3-encode explanation that accounts for the sky's raw-vs-corrected hue shift does NOT extend to gun's residual, since this whole comparison is already the P3->sRGB-corrected reading of the SAME frozen frame on both sides (no phase difference for (b)'s aliasing mechanism to apply to). [Final-review fix round, 2026-07-20 -- disclosed in .superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md Sec 6.1/6.2 but omitted from this entry's original text:] this verdict rests on ONE captured pose (frame 600); two further attempts (frames 400, 800) produced valid engine GPU dumps but the screencapture came back BLACK both times -- the display slept and then the session locked mid-session, and `caffeinate -dis` did not survive the lock, so a second pose was never obtained. This is the path (ii) that Task 4 could not exercise (getImageData blank in headless); it is now measured -- once -- and mostly clean, with the gun residual reported rather than folded into the clean verdict.

src/game/player.c:274 + src/game/unk_092E50.c:51 (Task 6, residual (b) SKY HUE -- REFUTED). The Dam sky is a scrolling cloud layer: g_SkyCloudOffset += g_ClockTimer (player.c:272-278), consumed as a texcoord offset on every sky quad vertex (player.c:486-656), wrapping at 4096 and never reset. It is therefore driven by the LEVEL-LOCAL clock, and g_GlobalTimer accumulates the identical delta on the identical frames -- so --screenshot-game-timer (not --screenshot-frame) is the correct pin. Native sky curve, captured at a pinned 4:3 640x480 window at RenderScale 1 across game timers 150..1400 (36 samples): sky HUE spans only 215.21..217.89 deg (2.68 deg total) while SATURATION spans 0.29..0.70. Three web GPU surface dumps were located on that curve by least squares over two sky ROIs (6 channels): web frame 400 -> native timer 234, joint rms 0.175 levels, dHue +0.028/+0.014 deg; frame 600 -> timer 1039, rms 0.016 levels, dHue +0.000/-0.003 deg; frame 800 -> timer 471, rms 0.007 levels, dHue +0.000/-0.008 deg. Every fit is unambiguous (nearest rival branch >=80 ticks away scores rms 2.4-6.2). Frame-invariant terrain controls agree independently: rock delta [-0.02,-0.02,-0.02], ground [+0.00,-0.00,+0.00]. => The web sky lies EXACTLY ON the native sky locus. There is no hue divergence; the historical 'web renders a more saturated blue' reading was a phase artifact, since saturation swings 0.29->0.70 over the scroll cycle while hue barely moves. METHOD CAVEAT (added Final-review fix round, 2026-07-20): this native curve is only valid from a COLD BOOT of the level -- g_SkyCloudOffset is never reset (accumulate + wrap only, player.c:272-278) while g_GlobalTimer zeroes at level init, so the timer<->sky-phase pin silently breaks after a level transition (e.g. reload-in-place, or arriving at Dam via a level-select chain rather than a fresh boot); all curve captures behind this entry were taken from a cold boot.

.superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md (Task 6, near-miss method note, Final-review fix round 2026-07-20). Task 6's FIRST pass at residual (b) appeared to show a genuine web-vs-native sky divergence, purely because the native reference curve was initially sampled only over game timers 150-750 (one hump of the periodic scroll) -- the web frame-600 dump did not fit anywhere on it, indistinguishable in shape from a real divergence. Lesson: a TOO-SHORT reference curve looks exactly like a real one. Correct handling, in order: (i) first re-ran the dump to rule out a torn read (byte-identical -> not torn) BEFORE concluding anything was wrong; only then (ii) extended the curve to 1400 -- and that extension was MECHANISM-DRIVEN, not fit-until-it-fits: the wrap-at-4096 accumulator (player.c:272-278) itself PREDICTS further periodic humps, so extending the sampled range followed from the mechanism, not from a post-hoc search for agreement. Final fit rms 0.016 (frame 600) vs the nearest rival branch's rms 4.54; two of the three web dumps' best-fit native timers (234 and 471, for frames 400 and 800) in fact land INSIDE the original 150-750 range -- only the frame-600 dump (best-fit timer 1039) actually required the extension. Filed here (FID-0134) rather than FID-0139 because the near-miss is specific to this finding's own sky-hue curve methodology, not a general web-capture instrumentation gap. Full account: .superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md Sec 6.2 item 2.

src/platform/platform_sdl.c:737 (Task 6, FID-0135 GEOMETRY AUDIT of this record's own Task-4 numbers -- numbers CORRECTED, conclusion UNCHANGED). Task 4's preserved captures re-measured: its NATIVE bitmaps are 640x480 with 75px bars = content 640x330, aspect 1.939 (the default 16:9 window squeezed into the fixed 640x480 screenshot canvas); its WEB captures are 640x393 with 16/15px bars = content 640x362, aspect 1.768. Task 4 therefore compared frames whose content aspects differ by 9.7%, with hand-placed whole-image-fraction ROIs in two different framings. The cost is large and falls almost entirely on the sky: at a FIXED game timer 300, changing ONLY the native window aspect moves the sky ROI from [61.1,75.5,98.1] (16:9 default) to [92.9,108.7,135.2] (4:3) -- a +32/+33/+37 level swing from capture geometry alone, the same order as the ~20-level 'sky lift' Task 4 was investigating. Re-measured at MATCHED geometry (native and web both 640x480, content 640x442, aspect 1.448) and matched phase, web-vs-native opaque-surface agreement is rock [-0.02,-0.02,-0.02] and ground [+0.00,-0.00,+0.00] -- roughly 100x tighter than Task 4's reported '<=2-3 levels in both directions', which was registration noise rather than real signal. Video.RenderScale (native 2 vs web 1) was measured and is NOT a confound: 4:3 RenderScale 2 vs RenderScale 1 differ by <=0.2 levels on every ROI.

docs/audit/DAM_RENDER_DEEP_DIVE_2026-07-18.md (CORRECTION 2026-07-20 closure paragraph + Fix round 1 corrections 2026-07-21 + CLOSURE round 2 2026-07-20 Task 6); full numeric table + harness in Evidence root task-4 (.superpowers/sdd/task-4-report.md) and task-6 (.superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md, harness .superpowers/sdd/task-6-harness/)

history.evidence: task-4 webprobe.mjs four-way read (native / CDP / GPU-scene-dump / GPU-surface-dump), dam --deterministic, HEAD 3f376bb.
history.note: REFUTED (Task 4). Built a discriminator that reads the SAME web frame three ways (CDP screenshot + two engine GPU-buffer readbacks that bypass canvas/compositor/CDP entirely) plus a wgpu-native baseline. Findings: (1) CDP == in-page GPU readback to a mean of <0.53 level/channel (rock/ground byte-identical) -> the historically-blamed CDP capture is faithful, NOT a color-management artifact. (2) Opaque 3D surfaces (rock/ground/viewmodel) are at native parity within <=2-3 levels in BOTH directions -> no systematic over-bright. (3) The prior ~7-11/~20-level 'lift' was pose/frame mismatch: the sky ROI swings >40 levels frame-to-frame on native alone as the idle camera sweeps the rock silhouette; the audit's web sky value lies inside native's own range. Docs-only closure; no engine change (default rendering untouched). The one residual is a small sky HUE shift (web slightly more saturated blue, i.e. bluer not brighter) that is real-in-bytes (CDP==dump) but pose-confounded and NOT the over-bright symptom; left as a minor open note, plausibly the known WebGPU sky-backdrop blend behavior (BLEND-1/FMA-2), present on wgpu-native too.

history.evidence: Fix round 1 correction: re-ran roi.mjs/diff.mjs directly on the preserved task-4 evidence bitmaps (shots/, web1/, web2/, web3/) against the closure's own numbers; all reviewer-flagged discrepancies confirmed.
history.note: [Timestamp corrected, Final-review fix round 2026-07-20: this entry was erroneously stamped 2026-07-21T00:00:00Z (a pre-existing typo from the prior task, one day ahead of the Task-6 entry below it despite chronologically preceding it); corrected to same-day. No content changed.] [SUPERSEDED IN PART by the 2026-07-20 round-2 entry below: fix (3)'s 'compositor path remains OPEN' caveat is now CLOSED, and the numeric parity figures cited here are corrected at matched geometry.] CORRECTED, status unchanged (refuted, scope narrowed). Four fixes to the 2026-07-20 record: (1) CRITICAL -- the '>40-level native sky swing from idle camera drift' mechanism and its cited f120/f200/f300/f350 numbers do NOT reproduce and are RETRACTED; the actual re-derived values show a STATIC camera (rock/ground byte-identical every frame) with a TIME-VARYING sky (monotonic +18 to +33 level/channel climb over 230 frames) -- this still invalidates cross-frame sky comparisons, but the mechanism is an animated sky term, not camera motion. (2) An undisclosed capture-sync failure in 1 of 3 web runs (web1: CDP-vs-dump full-image diff ~R3.3/G3.0/B2.5, vs <=0.53 on the clean run) is now disclosed as a method-reliability caveat; rock/ground stayed byte-identical even in the failed run, so the colorimetric conclusion survives (the failure is temporal, not colorimetric). (3) The verdict scope is narrowed: parity is proven only up to the GPU swapchain buffer; the canvas/compositor/display color-management path a human viewer sees was never exercised (path (ii) getImageData blank in headless) and remains OPEN, not verified. (4) The second pose (frame 250) CDP-vs-dump sky difference of ~2-3 levels is now noted explicitly rather than folded into 'CDP==dump'. The refuted status on the over-bright claim for opaque 3D surfaces stands; the finding's scope and the sky mechanism are corrected. Full derivation: .superpowers/sdd/task-4-report.md 'Fix round 1 -- corrected evidence'. Current-status caveat (folded from a prior status_note field): refuted holds for opaque 3D surfaces only -- the canvas/compositor/display color-management path a human viewer actually sees was never exercised and remains open, not verified; do not read this record's 'refuted' status as covering that path.

history.evidence: Task 6 (fix/tier1-residuals): headful-Chrome compositor capture vs GPU surface dump at a frozen frame (freeze verified byte-identical across 1.2 s), plus three web GPU dumps located on a 36-sample native sky curve pinned by --screenshot-game-timer over timers 150..1400, all at matched 640x480 4:3 geometry.
history.note: BOTH REMAINING RESIDUALS CLOSED; status stays refuted and the core claim is now much more strongly supported. [Caveats added in the Final-review fix round below: (a) rests on n=1 pose and its clean-verdict summary omitted the gun ROI -- see that entry.] (a) COMPOSITOR PATH -- CLOSED, clean. The path a human viewer actually sees was exercised for the first time: real Chrome window captured with macOS screencapture, compared against the engine's own GPU surface dump of the same frozen frame. After correcting the capture's Display P3 profile to sRGB, opaque surfaces agree to <=0.4 levels and the sky to <=2.2 levels with hue within 0.1 deg. The canvas/compositor/display path adds no brightness or hue shift. NOTE the trap this uncovered, filed as FID-0138: read RAW, the same capture shows a +3.0 to +3.6 deg sky hue shift and +6 to +8 levels of R, because screencapture tags its output Display P3 while neutrals stay untouched -- precisely the shape of a false 'web is bluer' positive. (b) SKY HUE -- REFUTED. Root-caused the time-varying sky to g_SkyCloudOffset += g_ClockTimer (player.c:272-278), a scrolling cloud texcoord consumed at player.c:486-656, wrapping at 4096 and never reset; it is driven by the level-local clock, so --screenshot-game-timer is the correct pin (--screenshot-frame is a render-frame counter and is NOT proportional to it -- see FID-0139). Across game timers 150..1400 the native sky hue spans only 215.21..217.89 deg while saturation spans 0.29..0.70, so hue is phase-robust and saturation is not. All three web dumps land ON the native locus: joint rms 0.175 / 0.016 / 0.007 levels over six sky channels with hue deltas <=0.028 deg, each fit unambiguous (rival branches score rms 2.4-6.2), and the frame-invariant terrain controls agree to <=0.02 levels. The 'web renders a more saturated blue' residual was a phase artifact and is retracted. (c) GEOMETRY AUDIT (FID-0135 applied to this record's own numbers): Task 4's native captures were taken at the default 16:9 window (content aspect 1.939) and its web captures at 1.768 -- a 9.7% mismatch. At a fixed timer, window aspect ALONE moves the native sky ROI by +32/+33/+37 levels, the same order as the effect under investigation. Task 4's numbers are therefore corrected: at matched geometry the web-vs-native opaque-surface agreement is [-0.02,-0.02,-0.02] (rock) and [0.00,0.00,0.00] (ground), about 100x tighter than the '<=2-3 levels in both directions' previously reported, which was registration noise. The REFUTED conclusion is unchanged and now rests on far tighter evidence. RenderScale (native 2 vs web 1) was checked and is not a confound (<=0.2 levels). Nothing in the engine was changed; this closure is docs-only.

history.evidence: Final-review fix round (fix/tier1-residuals): re-read .superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md Sec 6.1/6.2 (self-review) against the committed evidence text above and disclosed omissions the report already carried but the ledger/doc did not.
history.note: Final-review fix round, 2026-07-20. Two omissions in the committed record, both already present in the task report's own self-review but missing from what a reader of this ledger (without the report open) would see: (1) the (a) COMPOSITOR PATH closure rests on ONE captured pose (frame 600) -- two retry attempts (frames 400, 800) came back BLACK screencaptures after the display slept and the session locked (`caffeinate -dis` does not survive a lock); the 1.2s-apart byte-identity freeze-check proves TEMPORAL stability of that one frame, not pose coverage -- now stated directly in the (a) evidence entry above. (2) that entry's own compositor table includes a gun ROI (+1.63/+1.43/+1.31 P3-corrected) that the original closing summary omitted, instead stating 'opaque surfaces agree to <=0.4 levels' as if universal; gun is +1.6 levels and is reported as NOT isolated (suspected 2x2 box-downsample alignment on high-frequency viewmodel content) rather than folded into the clean verdict. Also newly recorded: a near-miss method note (new counter-log evidence entry, this file) on how a too-short native reference curve (150-750) made the frame-600 web dump look unmatchable until the curve was extended to 1400 on mechanism grounds (the wrap-at-4096 accumulator predicts further humps), not by fitting until it worked; and a method caveat that the sky curve is only valid from a cold boot (g_SkyCloudOffset never resets while g_GlobalTimer zeroes at level init). Status/conclusion UNCHANGED: refuted holds for both residuals; these are caveats on the strength/completeness of the evidence, not reversals. This closes the loop the branch's own earlier round had to retro-fit (see the 2026-07-20T09:00:00Z entry's disclosure history) by stating the report's caveats in the ledger the first time, rather than after review.
